title: Livestock Panic
description: Passive farm animals develop fight-or-flight responses and will scatter or defend themselves when predators or hostile mobs approach.
body:
# Livestock Panic

Livestock Panic introduces realistic survival instincts to passive farm animals. Chickens, sheep, cows, and pigs now detect nearby hostile mobs or wolves within a configurable radius and react accordingly.

## Animal Behaviors

- **Chickens** frantically scatter in random directions with increased speed
- **Sheep** group together and flee as a herd
- **Cows** occasionally charge and knockback smaller threats like spiders or silverfish
- **Pigs** squeal loudly, alerting nearby animals and potentially attracting players to investigate

## Features

- Animals return to normal behavior once threats leave the area
- Works seamlessly with vanilla breeding and farming mechanics
- Uses efficient scoreboard-based proximity detection
- Runs checks only every 20 ticks to minimize performance impact
- No commands required for basic functionality

## Configuration

Optional command `/trigger livestock_panic_range` allows players to adjust detection radius between 8-24 blocks.

## Use Cases

Players can intentionally use this behavior for automated mob sorting or must consider predator threats when building farms.
